config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena itu membuka ruang penyalahgunaan oleh negara atau mayoritas. Hak individu harus tetap dilindungi sebagai batas moral dan hukum, terutama untuk kebebasan berpendapat, privasi, dan perlakuan adil. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika kepentingan umum dicapai tanpa mengorbankan martabat orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The rights of the individual should not be treated as secondary to society in general, because in the United States that is exactly how you end up justifying abuse by governments, majorities, or institutions. I do think society has real interests, especially in public safety and public health, but those interests need to be limited by strong rights to speech, privacy, due process, and equal treatment. If you flip the principle and say society comes first by default, you make it too easy to silence unpopular people or punish minorities for the comfort of the majority. A healthy society is one that protects individual rights precisely so the collective does not trample them.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman sebagai orang Indonesia, saya justru melihat bahaya ketika “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk membenarkan pembungkaman, diskriminasi, atau keputusan sepihak oleh negara maupun mayoritas. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat pembatasan yang jelas, proporsional, dan adil terhadap hak individu, bukan dengan menempatkan individu sebagai korban yang wajar. Masyarakat yang sehat bagi saya adalah yang kuat justru karena melindungi martabat dan kebebasan tiap orang, termasuk yang tidak populer.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter, but they should not automatically outrank individual rights. From a U.S. perspective, I do not trust any government, majority, or institution to decide when it is acceptable to brush aside speech, privacy, due process, or equal treatment just because it claims to be acting for the common good. That kind of logic has too often been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and abuse against unpopular people. A legitimate society balances collective needs with firm limits, and those limits are the rights of the individual.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena di Indonesia frasa “demi kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menutupi penyalahgunaan kuasa, baik oleh negara maupun mayoritas. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh jadi alasan otomatis untuk mengorbankan kebebasan berpendapat, privasi, dan perlakuan adil. Justru kalau hak individu dilindungi dengan kuat, kepentingan bersama bisa dicapai tanpa menjadikan orang yang tidak populer sebagai korban. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang baik bukan yang paling keras memaksa, melainkan yang paling adil membatasi kekuasaan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society matter, but they should never be treated as automatically more important than individual rights. From an American perspective, I am very wary of any argument that says "the common good" can override speech, privacy, due process, or equal treatment, because that is exactly how governments and majorities justify overreach. Society works best when it sets narrow, fair limits for genuine public needs while keeping strong protections for unpopular individuals. If the collective can always win by default, then rights stop being rights and become privileges.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
